title: agalinis humilis
intro: Agalinis humilis, also known as low agalinis or small bugleweed, is a plant species that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. It is a hemiparasitic plant, meaning that it derives some of its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants, often using them as a host to obtain essential nutrients. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits such as wound healing and the treatment of respiratory issues. Agalinis humilis can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support the immune system and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
